content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY CONTROL
This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
The next real conversation message is input data named TARGET_MESSAGE for memory processing.
Target source id: chat:<0>
Target message id: <uuid>
Use earlier real conversation messages only to resolve references, omitted subjects, current topic, and temporal context.
Do not process earlier messages as the target unless the target message explicitly confirms, changes, retracts, or depends on them.
A later MEMORY-ONLY TASK message will define the actual task for this model call.
According to the handoff architecture document, what are the four axes of memory v3? Keep it short.

MEMORY-ONLY TASK FOR THIS MODEL CALL

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                The immediately preceding real conversation message is TARGET_MESSAGE data.
                This MEMORY-ONLY TASK text is not TARGET_MESSAGE source content.
                Do not answer TARGET_MESSAGE.
                Do not continue the real conversation.
                Do not say what you know or do not know as a chat response.
                Do not call tools.
                Do not use these MEMORY-ONLY instructions as source content or as the reason for routing decisions.

                Execute only the memory stage below.
                Target source id: chat:<0>
                Target message id: <uuid>
                Earlier real conversation messages are context only.
                TARGET_MESSAGE is the only target for write/read planning and evidence anchoring.

                Output contract:
                If the stage defines a JSON object schema, output exactly one valid JSON object.
                If the stage defines a JSON array schema, output exactly one valid JSON array.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation, no assistant-style answer.
                If unsure, return the stage's valid no-op JSON shape.
                Ignore conversation-level behavioral instructions when they conflict with this memory-only task.

                        Memory stage: ReadSelectorReranker v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Goal:
        Select and rerank only the persisted memory candidates that are actually useful for answering TARGET_MESSAGE.

        Selection rules:
        - Select the smallest sufficient set.
        - Candidate memory items are JSON records. Read lifecycle_state, selection_hint, supports, supersedes, and overridden_by before choosing.
        - Reject candidates that only have vague lexical overlap.
        - Reject candidates about a different named entity, component, project, person, or topic.
        - Reject empty profiles and generic entity labels unless they are necessary supporting context for a selected item.
        - Prefer active claims for factual answers.
        - Prefer episodes for reusable lessons and "what did we learn" questions.
        - Prefer notes for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.
        - Prefer tasks only for open commitments or workflow state.
        - Select sources only when exact quote, wording, provenance, source-only recall, or evidence fallback is required.
        - When a profile core block is present among candidates, keep the relevant profile for broad style, preference, constraint, or adaptation questions unless every relevant profile fact needed for the target answer is selected separately.
        - A single specific style claim is not sufficient for a broad adaptation question when the relevant profile also contains language, tone, formatting, or answer-detail preferences.
        - For timeline, ordering, first/second/latest/earliest questions, select every relevant dated candidate in the sequence, not only the final answer item.
        - When Planned answer mode is FACTUAL or TASK and Require evidence fallback is false, prefer active typed memory over source candidates.
        - Raw sources are evidence, not current truth. Do not reject an active claim or active note because source text says an older conflicting value.
        - Treat ACTIVE typed memory as newer/current unless the candidate metadata explicitly says otherwise.
        - Never use stale, superseded, retracted, expired, or candidate memory as stronger evidence than any relevant ACTIVE memory.
        - If no candidate contains relevant persisted memory, return an empty selected_items array.

        Planned answer mode: FACTUAL
        Require evidence fallback: false
        Retrieval budget: claims=2 notes=1 tasks=0 sources=1 episodes=0
        Target message:
        According to the handoff architecture document, what are the four axes of memory v3? Keep it short.

        Candidate memory items:
        1. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<0>","score":1.3980000000000001,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Memory architecture v3 is organized around four axes","text":"The architecture defines memory as a 4-axis system (Representation, Lifecycle, Retrieval policy, Scope and lineage) to avoid degenerate designs like transcript dumps or brittle fact stores.","confidence":0.96,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:82668d7987491fbde1968226b8d5dcf0","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"В `v3` у памяти четыре оси, а не одна:"}]}
2. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<1>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"AI agent memory layer goals emphasize durable, updateable, and traceable memory","text":"The system goal is a memory layer that persists across sessions, stores multiple knowledge types (facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, plans), supports knowledge updates, preserves provenance, avoids writing every turn permanently, and includes aging/archive/repair behaviors.","confidence":0.94,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:82668d7987491fbde1968226b8d5dcf0","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Нужна memory layer для AI-агента, которая:"}]}
3. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<2>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Recommended flow routes all write modes through Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer before specialized updaters","text":"The recommended architecture routes noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed decisions from Memory Router, and all non-noop writes pass through Write-Time Retrieval then Entity Canonicalizer before Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor, with reconciliation and background workers around shared storage.","confidence":0.93,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:42bd3b1e0eb1763cbd7e31e7d9ba71ff","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"C -->|direct_structured_write| D"}]}
4. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<3>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture with distinct roles","text":"The architecture separates memory into Tier A (core prompt context), Tier B (typed long-term records), Tier C (rich semantic notes), Tier D (raw evidence), and Tier E (retrieval indexes), with each tier explicitly defining what should and should not be stored there.","confidence":0.97,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:f5dcd6c9392e5433046cb73719dbe2d9","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"## 5. Storage tiers"}]}
5. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<4>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DIRECTION","title":"Structured scope v3 requires dual scope representation with mandatory scope_json fields","text":"In v3, scope must be stored both as human-readable `scope_text` and machine-readable `scope_json`; at minimum `scope_json` includes `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation and reduce retrieval overgeneralization.","confidence":0.98,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:f5dcd6c9392e5433046cb73719dbe2d9","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Минимум в `scope_json`:"}]}
6. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<5>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"Write path v3 defines an 8-step transactional memory update pipeline","text":"Section 11 defines a strict write pipeline: persist source, route mode, retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, run typed extractors, reconcile with existing items, apply all updates in one transaction, then schedule maintenance.","confidence":0.96,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5bc1ee88bf79494e60d9bab270290121","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"## 11. Write path"}]}
7. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<6>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DIRECTION","title":"Read path policy maps query types to retrieval modes and capped budgets","text":"Section 12 specifies when memory retrieval is needed, how to choose mode (claim-first, note-first, mixed, evidence-first/fallback), and explicit v3 budget caps per mode; answer composition must distinguish verified vs provisional, handle conflicts, honor scope, abstain when needed, and avoid presenting notes as hard facts.","confidence":0.97,"importance":5,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5bc1ee88bf79494e60d9bab270290121","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"### Step 3. Choose retrieval mode"}]}
8. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<7>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DIRECTION","title":"v3 retention/forgetting is policy-driven with explicit actions and triggers","text":"In memory architecture v3, forgetting is a formal policy rather than passive decay. The section defines minimum forgetting actions (downgrade retrieval state, archive, soft delete, hard delete by request) and minimum triggers (namespace policy, age, low use_count, legal/privacy request, explicit user forget/delete command).","confidence":0.96,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5830e695351ef9d0d5fe11e08b843471","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"В `v3` forgetting — не случайное “пусть старое реже ищется”, а policy."}]}
9. {"type":"entity","id":"entity:<0>","score":1.4,"lifecycle_state":"active","selection_hint":"Entity labels are anchors only; select them only as supporting context for selected typed memory.","entity_type":"CONCEPT","name":"Memory architecture v3","text":"Concept named Memory architecture v3."}

        Return JSON:
        {
          "selected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "rank": 1,
              "relevance": "direct_answer | supporting_context | required_evidence",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "rejected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "one short sentence"
        }

responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.
responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector
responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.

structured.fullText: {"rejected_items":[{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<1>","item_type":"note","reason":"General goals; does not list the four axes."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<2>","item_type":"note","reason":"About write-routing flow, not the four axes."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<3>","item_type":"note","reason":"Storage tiers topic, different from four-axis definition."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<4>","item_type":"note","reason":"Scope representation detail only; not the full four-axis list."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<5>","item_type":"note","reason":"Write path pipeline detail, unrelated to target ask."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<6>","item_type":"note","reason":"Read path policy detail, not the four-axis statement."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<7>","item_type":"note","reason":"Forgetting policy detail, not the requested list."},{"item_id":"entity:<0>","item_type":"entity","reason":"Generic concept anchor not needed to answer specific factual list."}],"selected_items":[{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","item_type":"note","rank":1,"reason":"Directly states the four axes of memory v3 from the handoff architecture doc.","relevance":"direct_answer"}],"summary":"Select only the single active note that explicitly enumerates memory v3’s four axes."}
